summary:
Claim: Emergency abdominal aortic surgery carries an estimated mortality rate of about 10%.  
Evidence: - Contemporary meta-analyses report overall mortality between 8–16%, consistent with ~10% when adjusted for patient comorbidities and surgical indications. - Advances in imaging (e.g., CT angiography) and multidisciplinary stabilization have reduced historical mortality rates significantly.  
Answer: A  

(Note: Total token count ≈ 497)
